questions.question: What type of model is used for classification in Bayesian learning?
questions.explanation: Naive Bayes model is used for classification in Bayesian learning as it provides an efficient and probabilistic approach.

questions.question: What is the main assumption of Naive Bayes model for Multinomial features?
questions.explanation: The main assumption of Naive Bayes model for Multinomial features is that the features are independent of each other given the class label.

questions.question: What is the purpose of normalizing the probability estimates in Naive Bayes model?
options: C) To ensure the probabilities sum to 1
questions.explanation: Normalizing the probability estimates in Naive Bayes model ensures that the probabilities sum to 1, making it easier to interpret the results.

questions.question: What is the advantage of using Naive Bayes over other classification models for spam detection?
options: A) It is faster to train
B) It is more accurate for high-dimensional data
C) It is easier to interpret the results
questions.explanation: Naive Bayes has the advantage of being easier to interpret the results, making it suitable for spam detection where the features are not as complex as in other applications.

questions.question: What is the limitation of Naive Bayes model when the features are correlated?
options: B) It is more computationally expensive
C) It does not handle correlated features well
D) It is more prone to overfitting
questions.explanation: Naive Bayes model does not handle correlated features well, which can lead to inaccurate results when the features are not independent of each other.
